title: Staying reliable against a flaky API
prompt_md: The third-party payment API you depend on is sometimes slow and sometimes returns `5xx` errors. Which combination best keeps *your* service reliable?
options: Retry instantly in a tight loop until the call finally succeeds
Set a timeout on each call, retry transient failures with exponential backoff, and put a circuit breaker in front to fail fast during a sustained outage
Remove the timeout so slow calls always get a chance to complete
Cache the last successful response and never call the API again
explanation_md:
The standard trio is **timeout + retry-with-backoff + circuit breaker**. Timeouts bound how long any single call can hang. Exponential backoff retries transient blips without hammering a struggling service. A circuit breaker trips during a longer outage so you fail fast instead of piling up doomed calls.

Instant tight-loop retries make an overloaded service worse (a self-inflicted DoS). Removing the timeout lets a hung call tie up a worker indefinitely. Never calling the API again isn't viable for charging real payments.
